title: Great Barrier Reef Ecosystem
extract: The Great Barrier Reef, stretching over 2,300 kilometers along Australia's northeastern coast, represents the world's largest coral reef system comprising approximately 2,900 individual reefs and 900 islands. This UNESCO World Heritage site supports extraordinary biodiversity, hosting over 1,500 species of fish, 400 types of coral, and 240 species of birds. Recent monitoring data indicates that sea surface temperature anomalies of 1.5°C above historical averages have accelerated coral bleaching events, with mass bleaching episodes occurring in 2016, 2017, 2020, and 2024. Conservation efforts employing coral gardening techniques and selective breeding for heat-resistant genotypes show promise for ecosystem resilience.
